page: Microsoft Azure L96as_v4 is a storage and memory-focused server belonging to the Lasv4 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture with 48 physical cores and multithreading. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is a key feature, offering 23,038 GB of capacity with Premium Storage capabilities, managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the L96as_v4 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. It achieves top 10% rankings in multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory performance is highly efficient, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching 7,287.47 GB/sec, and large-block uncached read and write speeds categorized as strong. Database operations are highly performant, highlighted by Redis SET operations processing 122.77 million operations per second. Single-core performance and large-model LLM inference speeds fall into the average to strong categories.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the L96as_v4 offers high resource density by bundling massive local storage capacity with substantial memory and compute resources. This eliminates the need for external storage attachments for data-heavy operations. The server is optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, heavy multi-threaded processing, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure L96as_v4 is a high-density, storage-optimized server from the Lasv4 family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of RAM and 23,038 GB of local storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, as evidenced by outstanding stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core results. Database operations are exceptionally strong, with Redis SET operations reaching 122.77 million operations per second. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its massive bundled local storage and high memory-to-core ratio, making it highly suitable for large-scale database hosting, data warehousing, and intensive multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L96as_v4 is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 23,038 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L96as_v4 features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 23 TB local storage, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and compute platform featuring massive local capacity and top-tier multi-core performance for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
768.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Excellent fit for large-scale databases and data warehousing
